query: Swiss perch fillets Eglifilet in beer batter with Swiss potato salad Herdoepfelsalat

draft.title: Beer-Battered Eglifilet with Potato Salad
draft.description: Crispy Swiss perch fillets in a light beer batter served with traditional Herdoepfelsalat, a tangy Swiss potato salad.

steps.text: Boil the peeled potatoes in salted water until tender, about 15 minutes. Drain, rinse under cold water, and slice into 1 cm thick rounds.
In a bowl, whisk together white wine vinegar, vegetable oil, mustard, chopped onion, chives, salt, and pepper to make the dressing.
Toss the warm potato slices gently with the dressing and set aside to marinate at room temperature.
In a separate bowl, combine flour, baking powder, salt, and pepper. Gradually whisk in chilled beer until smooth and slightly thick batter forms.
Heat oil in a deep pan or fryer to 180°C. Pat the eglifilet dry with paper towels.
Dip each perch fillet into the beer batter, allowing excess to drip off, then carefully lower into hot oil.
Fry the fillets in batches for 3-4 minutes until golden and crispy. Remove with a slotted spoon and drain on paper towels.
Serve the beer-battered eglifilet immediately with the Herdoepfelsalat on the side.

warnings: Maintain oil temperature to avoid greasy batter.
Ensure fish is fresh and properly dried before battering.
